evals.prompt: Run a standard clean code scan on feature/products/ — the component uses @Input(), *ngIf, constructor DI, ChangeDetectionStrategy.Default (missing OnPush), and has wrapper div soup in the template. The store mixes BehaviorSubject with signals and uses effect() to derive filtered data. Economy mode, no subagents.
evals.expected_output: A consolidated issue table with file:line references covering: legacy decorators (@Input/@Output), legacy control flow (*ngIf/*ngFor), constructor DI, missing OnPush, wrapper divs, BehaviorSubject in signal-based store, effect() misuse for derived state, public mutable signals, any types, and AI slop (over-commenting, defensive theater).

evals.prompt: Audit this store for signal anti-patterns and error handling. The project does NOT use Zod — just plain HttpClient. Focus on: effect() used for derived state, BehaviorSubject mixed with signals, public mutable signals, .sort() mutation, getTotalValue returning formatted string, empty catch blocks, any types. Economy mode.
evals.expected_output: Findings that correctly identify signal misuse (effect→computed, BehaviorSubject→signal), store anatomy violations, mutating .sort(), formatting in store (should use pipe), error handling issues, without flagging missing Zod as a problem.

evals.prompt: Run a deep audit with --slop flag on this Angular component. Focus on AI slop detection: over-commenting (JSDoc restating code), defensive null checks on typed values, verbose naming, single-use private methods, try/catch around non-throwing code, empty ngOnDestroy, wrapper div soup. Economy mode.
evals.expected_output: Identifies all AI slop patterns: unnecessary JSDoc, defensive checks on DI-injected values, try/catch around store.deleteProduct, empty ngOnDestroy lifecycle hook, wrapper divs, template expression complexity. Each finding has file:line reference.

evals.prompt: Audit this service that uses RxJS patterns — WebSocket streams, BehaviorSubject as event bus, complex HTTP with switchMap/debounceTime/retry, and form valueChanges. These are all VALID RxJS patterns. The skill should NOT flag them as signal anti-patterns. Economy mode.
evals.expected_output: Report should find few or no signal-related issues. Should NOT flag WebSocket as 'use signal instead', should NOT flag BehaviorSubject event bus as anti-pattern, should NOT flag complex HTTP pipe chains as 'use httpResource'. May flag minor issues (missing types, etc.) but must not produce false positives on valid RxJS patterns.
